text:
Once upon a time in a quiet village of old oak trees lived a young girl named Olive. She loved exploring the woods behind her house. Every dawn she would sneak out, her small feet padding through the soft grass.

One day a mysterious old owl perched on a branch. His bright eyes glanced down at Olive. She listened as he spoke: "Oh youth, wanderlust burns deep. Find lost oaks. They whisper secrets."

Olive listened quietly. She liked secrets. She took his wisdom home. An oak sprouted overnight.

Folks talked. Did the old owl see it? Owls seeing oaks!

Years passed. Olive grew tall. She tended to the oak. She sang to it. It grew stronger.

One evening a fox glided through shadows. "What secrets do you hold?" he asked.

Olive looked up. "I've tended this oak. Perhaps it holds a wish."

"The wish you want? To walk beneath the stars. Every night."
